findings: Nested progress is commander-role-only (line 143 `[[ "$role" == "commander" ]]`). Regular agent rows (line 195) receive zero nested display — no per-worker pulse visible for non-commander agents.

Nested data sourced exclusively from `swarm-state.json` (line 145). If the file is absent, `nested` is silently empty string — no fallback warning or staleness indicator is rendered on the commander row.

All nested metrics are collapsed into a single inline string appended to the commander row (line 193). There is no indented sub-row or visual hierarchy; squads, workers, atoms, heartbeat, and phase all concatenate on one line — can overflow narrow terminals despite BAR_W clamping (line 98).

Heartbeat age is computed (lines 167–174) and shown as `hb Xs` but carries no color change or alert threshold — a commander that has been silent for minutes displays identically to a live one, providing no visual pulse distinction.

The label `full SS-250` (line 193) is hardcoded and does not reflect `squads_target`/`workers_target` from telemetry — if a commander reports a non-250 scale, the monitor still displays 'full SS-250'.

Warning conditions (lines 176–180) cover only two cases: zero squads launched outside the startup phases, and workers_launched < workers_target on a success status. A commander stuck mid-run with partial squads but non-success status emits no warning.

`print_collab_summary` (lines 57–67) invokes `is_metaswarm_run()` on every 5-second loop iteration (line 116), which spawns a Python3 subprocess reading `fleet.json` each cycle — minor but additive overhead at scale.

README line 130 documents that nested progress is written to `commanders/{commander_id}/swarm-state.json` + `child-agents.jsonl`, but the monitor never reads `child-agents.jsonl` — child-agent telemetry is unused by the display layer.

`ORPHAN_RECOVERY_AFTER=180` (line 25) is hardcoded and shared between orphan recovery logic and `commander_active` staleness detection (line 274) — no way to tune the pulse-absence threshold without editing the script.
source_refs: README.md:130 (child-agents.jsonl documented but unused by monitor)
